attacking_style: Attacks with 15-20km remaining on sustained climbs rather than explosive finishes, prioritizing tempo-based distancing over short punchy accelerations. Most effective when given freedom from breakaways or when marking attacks before counter-punching.
team_tactics: Primary role as high-mountain domestique setting prolonged tempo to isolate rivals before leaders attack. Team protects him defensively when in GC contention rather than having him dictate offensive racing. Given stage-hunting freedom only when leaders' positions are secure.
terrain_preference: Excels on long, steady altitude climbs (12-17% sustained gradients) where tempo strength dominates. High-altitude racing in mountain ranges appears to unlock strongest performances. Struggles on short, explosive steep ramps that reward anaerobic bursts over sustained power.
vulnerability: Cannot follow or respond on extremely steep, short climbs requiring explosive accelerations (lost 1:05 on compact steep gradients). Lacks finishing speed against pure puncheurs. When under GC pressure, requests slower pace rather than matching aggressive tempo—suggests mental or physical fragility as protected leader.
key_patterns: Won stages from breakaways on 17% gradients when given team freedom (2019, 2021 examples)
Sets prolonged tempo as domestique to isolate GC rivals before leader attacks (observed 2019, 2023)
Vulnerable on short steep climbs; lost significant time when gradients exceeded sustained climbing profile
Course knowledge advantage: solo attack with 20km on home Andorran roads produced stage win
Defensive when leading GC, requesting slower pace; offensive when supporting or stage-hunting
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Vuelta showed vulnerability under GC pressure (requesting slower pace, losing time on steep climbs) despite ultimately winning with team protection. Stronger as free-role climber than protected leader.
